emails.subject: Martin's Cooling — when the AC dies at 10pm
emails.body:
Hey Martin,

Martin's Cooling & Heating has great coverage around Naples — you clearly know residential HVAC.

One thing I keep seeing with owner-operated HVAC companies: when someone's AC dies at 10pm in July and they call around, they hire whoever picks up or calls back first. The quality conversation happens later. The job decision happens in 10 minutes.

I build AI response systems for HVAC companies that handle those after-hours calls — responds within 2 minutes, qualifies the issue, and texts you the details so you can decide whether to call back immediately.

I build and run the whole thing free for 2 weeks so you can see exactly what you're currently losing to voicemail before deciding if it's worth paying for.

Worth a 15-minute call?

William Marceau
Marceau Solutions
wmarceau@marceausolutions.com
emails.pain_point: Naples summer AC emergencies — homeowner calls 2-3 HVAC companies at 10pm and hires the first one to respond
emails.hook: HVAC emergencies are time-sensitive and emotional; response time beats price every time

emails.subject: Naples Realty Services — Saturday evening buyer calls
emails.body:
Hey Todd,

Came across Naples Realty Services while looking at local brokerages — solid presence in the Naples market.

Here's what I see with real estate: buyer inquiries spike on evenings and weekends, which is exactly when it's hardest to be available. A buyer sees a listing Saturday night, calls, gets voicemail — and calls the next agent on Zillow.

I build AI phone systems for real estate businesses that respond to those calls immediately — captures the buyer's details, answers basic questions about the listing, and sets a callback time.

I build and run the full system free for 2 weeks so you can see how many inquiries it catches before deciding if it's worth paying for.

Worth a quick call?

William Marceau
Marceau Solutions
wmarceau@marceausolutions.com
emails.pain_point: Buyer inquiries peak evenings/weekends — unanswered calls go to the next agent on Zillow immediately
emails.hook: Real estate lead value is high and lead patience is zero; same-night response wins the client

emails.subject: Gardner Plastic Surgery — after-hours consultation inquiries

Hey Paul,

One thing that's almost universal with elective surgery: patients research late at night when they finally have time to think about it. They call or fill out a form at 9pm. William Marceau

emails.subject: All Saints Eye Center — new patient appointment calls
emails.body:
Hey Alison,

Came across All Saints Eye Center while researching Naples eye care — consistent reviews, great local reputation.

Here's something I hear from eye care practices: patients with vision insurance that resets in January or after a deductible is hit tend to call in bursts. When those calls land on a busy front desk or after hours, a lot of them don't call back — they just find a LensCrafters that answers the phone.

I build AI intake systems for eye care practices that handle those calls immediately — schedules the exam, answers insurance questions, captures the new patient's information.

I build and run it free for 2 weeks so you can see exactly how many new patient calls it handles before you decide if it's worth paying for.

Would a 15-minute call make sense?

William Marceau
Marceau Solutions
wmarceau@marceausolutions.com
emails.pain_point: Insurance-driven patient call surges hit busy front desks — patients who can't get through quickly book at a competing optical chain
emails.hook: Eye care has predictable call surges (insurance resets, referrals) and patients will go to a chain if the independent doesn't answer fast

emails.subject: Gulfshore Animal Hospital — calls when the clinic is full
emails.body:
Hey Kim,

Gulfshore Animal Hospital has a strong reputation in Naples — pet owners clearly trust your team.

One thing I see consistently with busy veterinary practices: calls come in when the front desk is tied up with a full waiting room. The pet owner on hold or voicemail calls the next vet in their contacts — and once they establish care somewhere else, they rarely switch back.

I build AI intake systems for veterinary practices that handle those calls immediately — books appointments, answers FAQs, sends a follow-up text if a call goes unanswered.

I build and run the full system free for 2 weeks so you can see what you're losing to missed calls before committing to anything.

Worth a quick call?

William Marceau
Marceau Solutions
wmarceau@marceausolutions.com
emails.pain_point: Busy waiting room = front desk can't answer calls; pet owner moves to next vet and stays there long-term
emails.hook: Veterinary client relationships are long-term; one missed first call = years of lost revenue

emails.verdict: MAYBE — small operator, but reactive/emergency service = strong phone fit

emails.subject: Luxe Naples Real Estate — luxury buyer inquiry response
emails.body:
Hey Inga,

Came across Luxe Naples Real Estate while researching the Naples luxury market — strong brand, clearly targeting the right buyers.

Luxury buyers move at their own pace, but when they're ready to talk, they want a response immediately. A missed call or slow reply from a top agent pushes them toward whoever answers — and in the $2M+ market, one missed conversation is a significant loss.
